sanskrit:
अहं क्रतुरहं यज्ञः स्वधाहमहमौषधम्।
मन्त्रोऽहमहमेवाज्यमहमग्निरहं हुतम्॥
transliteration:
ahaṃ kratur ahaṃ yajñaḥ svadhāham aham auṣadham |
mantro ’ham aham evājyam aham agnir ahaṃ hutam ||
literal_meaning: I am the Vedic ritual, I am the sacrifice, the ancestral offering, the healing herb, the mantra, the clarified butter, the fire, and the oblation itself.
contextual_meaning: Every component of action—intention, effort, instrument, and outcome—is a manifestation of the same underlying Self, dissolving the subject-object split that fuels performance anxiety and moral fatigue.
psychological_state: Post-peak-experience integration: the felt sense that one is not merely doing life, but that life is spontaneously doing itself through me.
trigger_scenario: After nailing a presentation you realize the prep, the adrenaline, the slides, the applause—even the coffee—were all expressions of the same aware presence that is reading these words right now.
anchor_text: Every part of the act is already the whole Self, so just play your role and relax.
